title: K6: recall omitted_candidate_count → structured dropped[]{id,reason}
description: Replace the bare numeric `omitted_candidate_count?: number` with structured `dropped?: { id: string, reason: 'retrieval_budget' | 'payload_budget' }[]` across the recall/plan-context contract, so the LLM sees WHICH candidates were dropped and WHY (controlled enum) instead of a naked count. Reuses the archive-scan dropped[]{key,reason} convention (C-005) verbatim — archive-scan uses {session_id,reason}, recall uses {id,reason}. CRITICAL: must NOT destabilize trimToPayloadBudget's size-measurement feedback loop — the dropped[] array must NOT grow inside the `serialize` measurement closure (see action). Migrate ALL 16 references. Constraint C-005 (unified structured {id,reason}, no per-tool vocabulary), C-006 (rebuild shared + tsc green).
action: 1) packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts: co-locate a recall drop-reason enum near the archive-scan dropped[] (~line 572) — `const _recallDropReasonSchema = z.enum(['retrieval_budget','payload_budget'])` with a comment: 'shared {key,reason} omission convention — archive-scan dropped[] uses {session_id,reason}, recall uses {id,reason}'. Replace `omitted_candidate_count: z.number().int().nonnegative().optional()` at line 221 (planContextOutputSchema) AND line 508 (recallOutputSchema) with `dropped: z.array(z.object({ id: z.string(), reason: _recallDropReasonSchema })).optional()`. 2) packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts: change the PlanContextResult type at line 161 from `omitted_candidate_count?: number` to `dropped?: { id: string; reason: 'retrieval_budget' | 'payload_budget' }[]`. At line 467 the retrieval_budget drop set is `rankedCandidates` minus `topKCandidates` (by id) — compute it ONCE before the trim as `retrievalDropped: {id,reason:'retrieval_budget'}[]` (CONSTANT across trim iterations). 3) FEEDBACK-LOOP MITIGATION (the reason this was deferred): the `serialize` closure at line 530 is called by trimToPayloadBudget to MEASURE each candidate slice's serialized size; it currently embeds `omitted_candidate_count: totalOmitted` (a number). Do NOT put a growing dropped[] array into that closure — that makes the measured size grow non-monotonically and destabilizes the trim search. Instead keep a NUMERIC size-proxy inside serialize: emit `...(totalOmitted>0 ? { dropped_count: totalOmitted } : {})` (or account a fixed per-entry byte estimate) so per-slice size stays monotone. Build the REAL dropped[]{id,reason} ONLY for the final result (line 575-589): `dropped = [...retrievalDropped (constant), ...payloadDropped]` where payloadDropped is the set of candidate ids in `candidates` (pre-trim) minus `trim.items` (post-trim) with reason:'payload_budget', appended AFTER trim settles — never inside serialize. 4) packages/server/src/services/recall.ts line 210: replace `const omitted = planResult.omitted_candidate_count ?? 0` with `const omitted = (planResult.dropped ?? []).filter(d => d.reason === 'retrieval_budget').length`. 5) packages/server/src/index.ts line 223: MCP server-instructions string `omitted_candidate_count > 0` → reference dropped[] (e.g. 'a non-empty `dropped[]` means more exist — narrow your intent'). 6) Migrate the 7 plan-context.test.ts assertions + recall.test.ts to the dropped[] shape. 7) .fabric/AGENTS.md if it references omitted_candidate_count (current census shows the only AGENTS-adjacent ref is the index.ts server-instructions text).

criteria: RED FIRST: flip recall.test.ts:300 (the 'surfaces omitted_candidate_count' test) to assert `result.dropped` is an array containing exactly one `{ id, reason: 'retrieval_budget' }` (replacing `expect(result.omitted_candidate_count).toBe(1)`), AND flip one plan-context.test.ts assertion (e.g. line 404) to the dropped[] shape — run BEFORE impl so both fail.
node/Grep zero-residual: `grep -rn 'omitted_candidate_count' packages/shared/src packages/server/src .fabric/AGENTS.md` returns ZERO (Grep tool / node fs, NOT bash ugrep).

planContextOutputSchema (api-contracts.ts:221) and recallOutputSchema (:508) carry `dropped: z.array(z.object({id:z.string(),reason:_recallDropReasonSchema})).optional()`; _recallDropReasonSchema = z.enum(['retrieval_budget','payload_budget']) is co-located near the archive-scan dropped[] (~572) with the shared-convention comment.

FEEDBACK-LOOP GUARD: the serialize measurement closure (plan-context.ts:530) does NOT carry a growing dropped[] array — it carries a numeric proxy (dropped_count) or a fixed per-entry estimate; add a test that a LARGE omission set (e.g. 50 candidates, top_k small + tight payload_budget) still trims to a stable result without oscillation (trimToPayloadBudget returns a settled trim.items, dropped[] reason counts are correct).

recall.ts:210 derives the hint count via `(planResult.dropped ?? []).filter(d => d.reason === 'retrieval_budget').length`; the existing next_steps hint string still fires when retrieval_budget drops occur.
index.ts:223 server-instructions text references dropped[] (no `omitted_candidate_count` literal remains in index.ts).
All 7 plan-context.test.ts assertions (lines 404,720,834,847,883,907,936) migrated to dropped[] shape and green.
`pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` succeeds (run BEFORE server tests — schema changed).
fabric-server vitest green (recall.test.ts + plan-context.test.ts incl. the new large-omission stability test).
convergence.verification: cd packages/shared && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build; cd ../.. && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test; node -e 'const cp=require("child_process");const fs=require("fs");const files=cp.execSync("git ls-files packages/shared/src packages/server/src .fabric/AGENTS.md",{encoding:"utf8"}).split("\n").filter(Boolean);let n=0;for(const f of files){const t=fs.readFileSync(f,"utf8").split("\n");t.forEach((l,i)=>{if(l.includes("omitted_candidate_count")){console.log(f+":"+(i+1));n++;}});}console.log("RESIDUAL omitted_candidate_count:",n);'
convergence.definition_of_done: recall and plan_context responses report omissions as a structured dropped[]{id,reason} list with a controlled reason enum (retrieval_budget | payload_budget) reusing the archive-scan convention, the LLM can see which candidates were dropped and why, the trimToPayloadBudget search remains stable under large omission sets, and no `omitted_candidate_count` literal survives anywhere in shared/server/AGENTS.

files.change: Add _recallDropReasonSchema=z.enum(['retrieval_budget','payload_budget']) near archive-scan dropped[] with shared {key,reason} convention comment; replace both omitted_candidate_count fields with dropped: z.array(z.object({id:z.string(),reason:_recallDropReasonSchema})).optional().

files.target: PlanContextResult type(161), omittedCandidateCount(467/540/582), serialize closure(530), final result(575-589)
files.change: Type→dropped[]; compute retrievalDropped once pre-trim from rankedCandidates minus topKCandidates (reason:'retrieval_budget'); serialize closure keeps NUMERIC dropped_count proxy only; build real dropped[] in final result = [...retrievalDropped, ...payloadDropped] where payloadDropped = candidates(pre-trim) minus trim.items ids (reason:'payload_budget'), appended after trim settles.
Replace `omitted_candidate_count > 0` wording with a non-empty dropped[] reference.

Migrate assertions to dropped[] shape; add a test that a large candidate corpus + tight payload_budget trims to a settled result with correct retrieval_budget/payload_budget reason counts (feedback-loop guard).
RED-first: assert result.dropped contains {id, reason:'retrieval_budget'}; keep the next_steps hint assertion.
Update to dropped[] vocabulary if present (census currently shows the only adjacent ref is the index.ts server-instructions text — verify and migrate if found).

implementation: RED: flip recall.test.ts:300 to assert dropped[]{id,reason:'retrieval_budget'} and flip plan-context.test.ts:404 to dropped[] shape; run server tests → fail.
Edit api-contracts.ts: add _recallDropReasonSchema near archive-scan dropped[]; replace both omitted_candidate_count fields with dropped[] optional.

Edit plan-context.ts: type→dropped[]; compute retrievalDropped once pre-trim; keep numeric dropped_count proxy inside serialize closure (NO growing array); build final dropped[] = retrievalDropped + payloadDropped(post-trim) at result assembly.
Edit recall.ts:210 consumer to derive count from dropped.filter(reason==='retrieval_budget').
Edit index.ts:223 server-instructions string to reference dropped[].
Migrate the 7 plan-context.test.ts assertions; add the large-omission stability test (feedback-loop guard).
GREEN: server vitest green; `pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit` 0; node residual census = 0.
Commit on a feature branch; open PR (PR #2 of 2, independent of TASK-001).

success_metrics: dropped[]{id,reason} replaces count in both schemas
server vitest green incl 7 migrated assertions

reference.pattern: Reuse the archive-scan dropped[] shape (api-contracts.ts ~572: z.array(z.object({session_id, reason:z.enum([...])}))) — same {key,reason} convention, recall keying on id.

rationale.chosen_approach: Structured dropped[]{id,reason} reusing the archive-scan convention (C-005) gives the LLM actionable drop reasons with zero new vocabulary. Separate PR isolates the wide migration + the subtle trim feedback-loop risk.
decision_factors: feedback-loop: serialize closure must stay monotone
rationale.tradeoffs: dropped[] is larger on the wire than a number, but only present when truncation fires (optional), and the size-measurement closure uses a numeric proxy so trim sizing stays stable.

risks: TRIM FEEDBACK LOOP (deferral reason): putting a growing dropped[] inside the serialize measurement closure (plan-context.ts:530) makes measured payload size grow non-monotonically and destabilizes trimToPayloadBudget. MITIGATION enforced by criteria: serialize carries only a numeric proxy; retrieval_budget dropped[] computed once pre-trim (constant); payload_budget entries appended to the FINAL result only, after trim settles. Add the large-omission stability test.
WIDE MIGRATION SURFACE: 16 references across schema/producer/consumer/tests/server-instructions — use node/Grep residual census (=0), NOT bash ugrep (false negatives).
SHARED SCHEMA: forgetting `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` after api-contracts.ts edit → runtime invalid_union_discriminator. Rebuild BEFORE server tests.
tsc gate: local tsup --dts ≠ CI tsc --noEmit — run `pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit` before push.
